mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations and generating positive forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, prompting equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between residential construction starts and consumer credit outstanding indicates a structural shift in household leverage and housing demand, which drives equity repricing over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST minus RSAFS normalized) shows no statistically significant relationship with forward returns (p > 0.10) across a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing employment growth signals rising productivity and margin expansion, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The productivity spread (INDPRO/PAYEMS ratio) fails to predict quarterly returns with an out-of-sample R-squared greater than 0.05, or direction flips consistently in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands corporate revenue expectations and compresses equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth shows no monotonic relationship with 5-day forward returns, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation exceeding the Federal Reserve's implicit target triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when inflation crosses the 3.0% threshold, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy over any 3-year rolling period, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts combined with weakening industrial production signals a broad-based demand shock, which forces equity de-risking and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when both series decline simultaneously, or the signal produces false breakouts in > 40% of historical instances.
